messages.content:
What was observed
- Host 192.168.0.12 could not reach 172.16.0.12 (0/25 pings).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Edge routers were missing routes for the opposite LANs, so traffic was not being forwarded between 192.168.0.0/24 and 172.16.0.0/24.

Changes made
- Added static route on R1: 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.12.2.
- Added static route on R2: 192.168.0.0/24 via 10.0.12.1.

Verification — passed/failed and what remains
- Passed: R1 -> 172.16.0.12 ping now 5/5 (100%); R1 and R2 running-configs contain the new static routes; inter-router ACLs allow IP traffic.
- Partial issue remaining: R2 -> 192.168.0.12 ping succeeded 4/5 (80% — 1/5 packet loss). Recommend checking host firewall on 192.168.0.12, interface error counters/physical link health, and ARP entries on R1/R2 to eliminate the intermittent loss.
